relevant_cases.verbatim_excerpt: A 60-year-old woman presented to the emergency department after a syncopal episode during which she struck her head. She reported the syncope was associated with acu
relevant_cases.relevance_notes: Vascular rupture causing hemodynamic collapse - syncope, BP drop to 75/60 mmHg, hemoglobin 7.8 g/dL. Matches pattern of post-procedural hypotension with hemoglobin drop from bleeding.

relevant_cases.verbatim_excerpt: A 92-year-old woman was referred to vascular surgery for an enlarging hematoma of the left thigh. Two months earlier, she had undergone dynamic hip screw fixation
relevant_cases.relevance_notes: Post-procedural vascular complication with progressive hematoma enlargement and hemoglobin decline (10.7 to 7.1 g/dL). Post-operative bleeding after orthopedic procedure with hemodynamic compromise.

relevant_cases.verbatim_excerpt: A 72-year-old woman with bilateral Crowe type IV developmental dysplasia of the hip and a history of mitral valve replacement on warfarin (1 mg daily) and aspirin
relevant_cases.relevance_notes: Hemorrhagic shock from femoral artery pseudoaneurysm - hemoglobin 5.1 g/dL, hypotension (93/58 mmHg), massive hematoma. Vascular access-site complication causing hemodynamic collapse.
